title: The clockmaker's last repair
summary: Tell the story of an elderly clockmaker and the last watch he ever repaired.
topic: An elderly clockmaker and his final repair
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences telling the story of an old craftsman doing their last piece of work. Say who the person is, what they did, and how the story ended. Use past simple.
hintStarters: Three months before he retired, Mr Tanaka received a small parcel in the post…
He placed the watch on the cloth and leaned forward to…
When the watch started ticking again, he sat back and…

hintVocab.meaningVi: thợ sửa đồng hồ đeo tay
lò xo chính trong đồng hồ cơ
đôi tay vững vàng không run
tinh tế, mỏng manh, cần sự khéo léo

sampleText: Three months before he retired, Mr Tanaka received a small parcel at his shop in Osaka. Inside was a pocket watch from 1923. The sender was an old woman in Kyoto. She wrote a short note: her grandfather received the watch as a wedding gift, and it stopped working fifty years ago. Nobody could fix it. She heard about Mr Tanaka from a neighbour. He placed the watch on a piece of white cloth and examined it with his magnifying glass. The case was tarnished but the mechanism inside was complete. He spent two full days cleaning each small gear with a cotton cloth and a little solvent. On the third day, he found a broken mainspring. He replaced it with a part he kept in a flat tin on the top shelf. He wound the watch carefully and set it down. After a few seconds, it began to tick. He wrote back to the woman in Kyoto and told her the watch was ready. She came to collect it herself. She was very old and walked slowly with a stick. She held the watch to her ear for a moment and did not say anything. Mr Tanaka did not charge her. He said she gave him a better last job than he deserved.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ba tháng trước khi ông nghỉ hưu
nhận được một gói hàng nhỏ

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ông nội của bà nhận được chiếc đồng hồ như một món quà đám cưới

sampleAnnotations.phrase: placed the watch on a piece of white cloth
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đặt chiếc đồng hồ lên một mảnh vải trắng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: examined it with his magnifying glass
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: kiểm tra nó bằng kính lúp
Vỏ đồng hồ bị xỉn màu
bộ máy bên trong vẫn còn nguyên vẹn
lau sạch từng bánh răng nhỏ
một lò xo chính bị gãy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: He replaced it with a part
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ông thay nó bằng một linh kiện
Ông lên dây cót đồng hồ cẩn thận
nó bắt đầu kêu tích tắc

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đưa chiếc đồng hồ lên tai

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một công việc cuối đời tốt hơn những gì ông xứng đáng được nhận

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for a story told in order
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi kể chuyện trong quá khứ, mỗi động từ past simple mô tả một hành động xảy ra sau hành động trước. Chú ý thứ tự: 'received → placed → examined → spent → found → replaced → wound → set → began to tick'. Thứ tự động từ = thứ tự thời gian.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When telling a past story, each past simple verb describes an action that follows the previous one. Notice the sequence: 'received → placed → examined → spent → found → replaced → wound → set → began to tick'. The order of verbs equals the order of time.

sampleTranslationVi: Ba tháng trước khi ông nghỉ hưu, ông Tanaka nhận được một gói hàng nhỏ tại cửa hàng của mình ở Osaka. Bên trong là một chiếc đồng hồ bỏ túi từ năm 1923. Người gửi là một bà lão ở Kyoto. Bà viết một mảnh giấy ngắn: ông nội của bà nhận được chiếc đồng hồ như một món quà đám cưới, và nó ngừng hoạt động năm mươi năm trước. Không ai sửa được. Bà nghe về ông Tanaka từ một người hàng xóm. Ông đặt chiếc đồng hồ lên một mảnh vải trắng và kiểm tra nó bằng kính lúp. Vỏ đồng hồ bị xỉn màu nhưng bộ máy bên trong vẫn còn nguyên vẹn. Ông mất hai ngày đầy đủ để lau sạch từng bánh răng nhỏ bằng vải cotton và một ít dung môi. Đến ngày thứ ba, ông tìm thấy một lò xo chính bị gãy. Ông thay nó bằng một linh kiện ông giữ trong một hộp thiếc phẳng trên kệ trên cùng. Ông lên dây cót đồng hồ cẩn thận và đặt xuống. Sau vài giây, nó bắt đầu kêu tích tắc. Ông viết thư lại cho bà ở Kyoto và nói rằng chiếc đồng hồ đã sẵn sàng. Bà đến lấy trực tiếp. Bà rất già và đi chậm bằng gậy. Bà đưa chiếc đồng hồ lên tai trong một khoảnh khắc và không nói gì. Ông Tanaka không tính tiền. Ông nói bà đã cho ông một công việc cuối đời tốt hơn những gì ông xứng đáng được nhận.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best sentence for the opening of this past story.
options: Three months before he retired, Mr Tanaka received a small parcel.
Three months before he retired, Mr Tanaka receives a small parcel.
Three months before he retired, Mr Tanaka has received a small parcel.
Three months before he retire, Mr Tanaka received a small parcel.
mcQuestions.explanation: A finished past event needs past simple throughout: 'retired' and 'received'. 'Receives' is present; 'has received' is present perfect; 'retire' is missing past form.
mcQuestions.hint: A finished past story uses past simple for every event in the sequence.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says how long Mr Tanaka spent cleaning?
options: He spent two full days to clean each small gear.
He spent two full days cleaning each small gear.
He spent two full days for cleaning each small gear.
He spent two full days in clean each small gear.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Spend time doing something' requires a gerund (-ing form), not an infinitive or preposition + base form.
mcQuestions.hint: What form follows 'spend time' to describe the activity?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about what Mr Tanaka found inside the watch.
options: He found a broken mainspring.
He founded a broken mainspring.
He find a broken mainspring.
He finds a broken mainspring.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Found' is the irregular past simple of 'find'. 'Founded' means 'established'. 'Find' and 'finds' are present tense.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past simple of 'find'? Be careful not to confuse it with 'founded'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'replace … with'.
options: He replaced the spring with a new part.
He replaced the spring by a new part.
He replaced the spring for a new part.
He replaced the spring to a new part.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Replace something with something' is the correct structure. 'By', 'for', and 'to' are all wrong with 'replace'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition follows 'replace … ___' when naming the substitute?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means a small flat case that holds a watch?
options: parcel
inscription
case
gear
mcQuestions.explanation: The 'case' is the outer covering or shell of a watch or clock. A 'parcel' is a wrapped package; an 'inscription' is engraved writing.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word describes the outer shell of a watch?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence about the woman arriving to collect her watch.
options: She came to collect it herself.
She came for collect it herself.
She came collecting it herself.
She came to collecting it herself.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Come to + infinitive' expresses purpose. 'To collect' is correct; 'for collect', 'collecting', and 'to collecting' are all wrong.
mcQuestions.hint: What structure expresses the purpose of coming ('in order to')?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about the woman holding the watch to her ear.
options: She held the watch to her ear.
She hold the watch to her ear.
She holded the watch to her ear.
She was hold the watch to her ear.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Held' is the irregular past simple of 'hold'. 'Holded' is incorrect. 'Was hold' is not a valid tense form.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past simple of 'hold'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means 'to coil the spring to give a mechanical watch energy'?
options: turn up the watch
wind up the watch
pull up the watch
press up the watch
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Wind up' a mechanical watch means to turn the crown to coil the mainspring and give it energy.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrasal verb describes tightening the spring in a mechanical watch?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence about what happened after the watch was wound.
options: After a few seconds, it began to tick.
After a few seconds, it begins to tick.
After a few seconds, it begun to tick.
After a few seconds, it begin to tick.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Began' is the irregular past simple of 'begin'. The story is in the past, so past simple is needed.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past simple of 'begin'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the phrase that correctly reports what Mr Tanaka told the woman.
options: He told her the watch was ready.
He told her the watch is ready.
He told to her the watch was ready.
He said her the watch was ready.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Tell someone + clause' is correct. Past tense shifts 'is' to 'was' in reported speech. 'Tell to' and 'say someone' are both ungrammatical.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'tell' take a direct object (person) before the clause? What tense goes in the reported clause?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best shows that someone worked with great precision and care?
options: He cleaned each small gear with a cotton cloth.
He cleaned some of the gears with a big brush.
He quickly wiped the gears with his sleeve.
He pushed the gears into place and closed the case.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Each small gear' and 'cotton cloth' signal precision and care. The other options describe speed, rough tools, or shortcuts.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option shows the most careful and precise method?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mr Tanaka did not charge the woman for his final repair. Do you think there are some things that should not have a price? Why or why not?
